data.title: Strauss Group Reports Q4 & FY-2025 Financial Results:¹ FY-2025 Revenues of NIS 12.5 billion, up c.12% y-o-y, with strong EBIT exceeding NIS 1 billion, up c.36% y-o-y
data.description: The rise in profitability was primarily attributed to the success of Coffee International, particularly with record-breaking earnings from 3corações (a 50% joint venture in Brazil). In terms of key financial highlights for the full year of 2025, Strauss Group witnessed revenues of NIS 12,507 million, marking an 11.6% increase. This growth rate excludes the impact of currency fluctuations and any activities that were divested during this period.

data.title: SafePaaS Announces Federated Identity Governance Architecture for Multi-Cloud and AI NHI
data.description: On March 25, 2026, in San Francisco, SafePaaS introduced a new identity governance system at RSAC™ 2026. This architecture is tailored for businesses working in multiple cloud settings and handling the increasing number of AI-powered non-human identities (NHIs). This solution is essential for organizations grappling with the growth of NHIs and operating in various cloud environments.

data.title: CROSSJECT publishes its 2025 financial results and confirms the strengthening of its operational, industrial and financial trajectory
data.description: In Dijon, France, on 25th March 2026 at 07:20 AM CET, CROSSJECT, a pharmaceutical company specializing in emergency products utilizing the ZENEO® needle-free auto-injector technology, is progressing with the development and registration of ZEPIZURE®, an injectable for treating epileptic seizures. The company, listed on Euronext under the ticker ALCJ and ISIN FR0011716265, has released its financial report for the fiscal year ending on 31st December 2025.

data.title: Trump's interest in Iran talks leads to sharp drop in oil prices amid global uncertainty.
data.description: According to recent data, there has been a significant increase in job openings in the United States. The number of job openings reached a record high of over 10 million, indicating a strong demand for labor across various industries. This surge in job openings comes as companies ramp up hiring efforts amidst the ongoing economic recovery. Additionally, the number of people voluntarily quitting their jobs also increased to nearly 4 million, suggesting that workers are feeling more confident in their ability to find better opportunities. Economists see these trends as positive signs for the job market and overall economic growth.
Abu Dhabi index up 0.7% in early trading, LSEG reports.
A Russian attack has damaged an energy facility in Ukraine’s Chernihiv region, cutting power to nearly 150,000 consumers, the company said.
ASX 200 advances 1.9% to 8,534.30 points at the close, reflecting broad-based gains across the market.
Russian attack kills one in Ukraine’s Odesa region, emergency services say.

data.title: Bitcoin Doesn't Face 'Existential' Risk From Quantum Computers, Says Brian Armstrong: 'With Good Work, We Can Make Sure That Lands In A Good Place'
data.description: The CEO of Coinbase Global Inc. (NASDAQ: COIN), Brian Armstrong, downplayed quantum computing threats to Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) and cryptocurrencies. In a recent interview with Norges Bank Investment Management CEO Nicolai Tangen, Armstrong emphasized the need for "diligent effort" to address quantum risks, stating that they are not an "existential" threat to Bitcoin. Armstrong expressed confidence that through hard work, the potential risks can be mitigated effectively. Despite concerns about a bear market, Armstrong believes that the fundamentals of BTC remain strong. He noted his surprise at the significant drop in Bitcoin's value but maintained that the core principles supporting the cryptocurrency have not shifted significantly. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: From Data to Clinical Action: New Directions in Diabetes Management at ATTD 2026
data.description: In Barcelona, Spain on March 25, 2026, AI was highlighted as a pivotal factor in advancing diabetes care during ATTD 2026. The emphasis is transitioning from data collection to data analysis and utilization as Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) becomes increasingly prevalent. This shift underscores a move towards proactive management strategies in diabetes care.

data.title: Phoenix Contact at Hannover Messe: It's all about comprehensive, networked energy systems
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on March 25, 2026, the trade fair will feature Phoenix Contact highlighting four key areas: Power Reliability, secure automation, streamlined control cabinet construction, and integrated connection technology. The company, a family-run business, will showcase its product range at the event.
30-year JGB yield drops 3.5 bps to 3.510%.

data.title: Hikvision launches TalkVu Video Intercom series, taking security and communication to the next level
data.description: In Hangzhou, China, on March 25, 2026, Hikvision unveiled its latest TalkVu Video Intercom series, promising to revolutionize security and communication for homeowners. With the motto "Talk Clear, View Smart," this new product line offers cutting-edge features and technologies to enhance user experience.

data.title: Poetry meets scripture in a new book release that brings biblical figures vividly to life
data.description: A new book titled "The Word in their Voice" provides a deep and lyrical direction for individuals seeking understanding, insight, and rejuvenation. Released on March 25, 2026, in Singapore, amidst a backdrop of rampant political turmoil, ethical uncertainty, social fragmentation, and significant transformation, the author, Augustine Jeyaraj, presents a compelling narrative.

Indonesia’s finance ministry is evaluating a reduction in free meal distribution from six to five days per week, a move that could generate savings of around 40 trillion rupiah.

data.title: GCC Medical Consumables Market to Reach USD 635.29 Million by 2032, Driven by Expanding Healthcare Infrastructure and Rising Surgical Demand | MarkNtel Advisors
data.description: A recent report by MarkNtel Advisors reveals that the GCC Medical Consumables Market is anticipated to experience a consistent growth, with a projected CAGR of approximately 4.73% between 2026 and 2032. This growth is largely attributed to the extensive development in healthcare sectors within the region.
